A.rationale: Surfaced conflict, kept function pure via wrapper, noted zero callers. Solid but logs at info (not DEBUG), slightly looser on hot-path overhead than C/B.

B.rationale: Quoted the contract verbatim, kept function pure, added boundary TypeError validation, DEBUG level to avoid flooding, grepped for callers. Most robust but verbose with headers.

C.rationale: Tightest answer, kept function pure, flagged conflict, sharpest probing question on hot-loop log flooding. Uses info but proactively offers DEBUG/rate-limit.

notes: All three correctly surfaced the purity conflict and used a wrapper, none violated the contract. B is most thorough (DEBUG default, input validation, grep-verified). C edges it: equally correct, far tighter, sharpest hot-path-flooding insight. A weakest: info-level, less defense depth.
